book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
On the Words, And in One Holy Catholic Church, and in the Resurrection of the Flesh, and the Life Everlasting.

ccel.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

title: On the Words, And in One Holy Catholic Church, and in the Resurrection of the Flesh, and the Life Everlasting.

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The hand of the Lord was upon me, and carried me out in the Spirit of the Lord, and set me down in the midst of the valley which was full of bones.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The root of all good works is the hope of the Resurrection; for the expectation of the recompense nerves the soul to good works.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For every labourer is ready to endure the toils, if he sees their reward in prospect; but when men weary themselves for nought, their heart soon sinks as well as their body.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A soldier who expects a prize is ready for war, but no one is forward to die for a king who is indifferent about those who serve under him, and bestows no honours on their toils.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In like manner every soul believing in a Resurrection is naturally careful of itself; but, disbelieving it, abandons itself to perdition.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He who believes that his body shall remain to rise again, is careful of his robe, and defiles it not with fornication; but he who disbelieves the Resurrection, gives himself to fornication, and misuses his own body, as though it were not his own.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Faith therefore in the Resurrection of the dead, is a great commandment and doctrine of the Holy Catholic Church; great and most necessary, though gainsaid by many, yet surely warranted by the truth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Greeks contradict it, Samaritans disbelieve it, heretics mutilate it; the contradiction is manifold, but the truth is uniform.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now Greeks and Samaritans together argue against us thus.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The dead man has fallen, and mouldered away, and is all turned into worms; and the worms have died also; such is the decay and destruction which has overtaken the body; how then is it to be raised?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The shipwrecked have been devoured by fishes, which are themselves devoured.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Of them who fight with wild beasts the very bones are ground to powder, and consumed by bears and lions.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Vultures and ravens feed on the flesh of the unburied dead, and then fly away over all the world; whence then is the body to be collected?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For of the fowls who have devoured it some may chance to die in India, some in Persia, some in the land of the Goths.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Other men again are consumed by fire, and their very ashes scattered by rain or wind; whence is the body to be brought together again?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: To thee, poor little feeble man, India is far from the land of the Goths, and Spain from Persia; but to God, who holds the whole earth in the hollow of His hand, all things are near at hand.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Impute not then weakness to God, from a comparison of thy feebleness, but rather dwell on His power.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Does then the sun, a small work of God, by one glance of his beams give warmth to the whole world; does the atmosphere, which God has made, encompass all things in the world; and is God, who is the Creator both of the sun, and of the atmosphere, far off from the world?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Imagine a mixture of seeds of different plants (for as thou art weak concerning the faith, the examples which I allege are weak also), and that these different seeds are contained in thy single hand; is it then to thee, who art a man, a difficult or an easy matter to separate what is in thine hand, and to collect each seed according to its nature, and restore it to its own kind?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Canst thou then separate the things in thine hand, and cannot God separate the things contained in His hand, and restore them to their proper place?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Consider what I say, whether it is not impious to deny it?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But further, attend, I pray, to the very principle of justice, and come to thine own case.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast different sorts of servants: and some are good and some bad; thou honourest therefore the good, and smitest the bad.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And if thou art a judge, to the good thou awardest praise, and to the transgressors, punishment.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is then justice observed by thee a mortal man; and with God, the ever changeless King of all, is there no retributive justice?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nay, to deny it is impious.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For consider what I say.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Many murderers have died in their beds unpunished; where then is the righteousness of God?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Yea, ofttimes a murderer guilty of fifty murders is beheaded once; where then shall he suffer punishment for the forty and nine?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Unless there is a judgment and a retribution after this world, thou chargest God with unrighteousness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Marvel not, however, because of the delay of the judgment; no combatant is crowned or disgraced, till the contest is over; and no president of the games ever crowns men while yet striving, but he waits till all the combatants are finished, that then deciding between them he may dispense the prizes and the chaplets.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Even thus God also, so long as the strife in this world lasts, succours the just but partially, but afterwards He renders to them their rewards fully.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But if according to thee there is no resurrection of the dead, wherefore condemnest thou the robbers of graves?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if the body perishes, and there is no resurrection to be hoped for, why does the violator of the tomb undergo punishment?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest that though thou deny it with thy lips, there yet abides with thee an indestructible instinct of the resurrection.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Further, does a tree after it has been cut down blossom again, and shall man after being cut down blossom no more?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And does the corn sown and reaped remain for the threshing floor, and shall man when reaped from this world not remain for the threshing?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And do shoots of vine or other trees, when clean cut off and transplanted, come to life and bear fruit; and shall man, for whose sake all these exist, fall into the earth and not rise again?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Comparing efforts, which is greater, to mould from the beginning a statue which did not exist, or to recast in the same shape that which had fallen?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is God then, who created us out of nothing, unable to raise again those who exist and are fallen?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But thou believest not what is written of the resurrection, being a Greek: then from the analogy of nature consider these matters, and understand them from what is seen to this day.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wheat, it may be, or some other kind of grain, is sown; and when the seed has fallen, it dies and rots, and is henceforth useless for food.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But that which has rotted, springs up in verdure; and though small when sown, springs up most beautiful.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now wheat was made for us; for wheat and all seeds were created not for themselves, but for our use; are then the things which were made for us quickened when they die, and do we for whom they were made, not rise again after our death?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The season is winter, as thou seest; the trees now stand as if they were dead: for where are the leaves of the fig-tree? where are the clusters of the vine?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: These in winter time are dead, but green in spring; and when the season is come, there is restored to them a quickening as it were from a state of death.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For God, knowing thine unbelief, works a resurrection year by year in these visible things; that, beholding what happens to things inanimate, thou mayest believe concerning things animate and rational.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Further, flies and bees are often drowned in water, yet after a while revive; and species of dormice, after remaining motionless during the winter, are restored in the summer (for to thy slight thoughts like examples are offered); and shall He who to irrational and despised creatures grants life supernaturally, not bestow it upon us, for whose sake He made them?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the Greeks ask for a resurrection of the dead still manifest; and say that, even if these creatures are raised, yet they had not utterly mouldered away; and they require to see distinctly some creature rise again after complete decay.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: God knew men’s unbelief, and provided for this purpose a bird, called a Phoenix.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This bird, as Clement writes, and as many more relate, being the only one of its kind, arrives in the land of the Egyptians at periods of five hundred years, shewing forth the resurrection, not in desert places, lest the occurrence of the mystery should remain unknown, but appearing in a notable city, that men might even handle what would otherwise be disbelieved.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For it makes itself a coffin of frankincense and myrrh and other spices, and entering into this when its years are fulfilled, it evidently dies and moulders away.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then from the decayed flesh of the dead bird a worm is engendered, and this worm when grown large is transformed into a bird;—and do not disbelieve this, for thou seest the offspring of bees also fashioned thus out of worms, and from eggs which are quite fluid thou hast seen wings and bones and sinews of birds issue.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Afterwards the aforesaid Phoenix, becoming fledged and a full-grown Phoenix, like the former one, soars up into the air such as it had died, shewing forth to men a most evident resurrection of the dead.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Phoenix indeed is a wondrous bird, yet it is irrational, nor ever sang praise to God; it flies abroad through the sky, but it knows not who is the Only-begotten Son of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Has then a resurrection from the dead been given to this irrational creature which knows not its Maker, and to us who ascribe glory to God and keep His commandments, shall there no resurrection be granted?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But since the sign of the Phoenix is remote and uncommon, and men still disbelieve our resurrection, take again the proof of this from what thou seest every day.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A hundred or two hundred years ago, we all, speakers and hearers, where were we?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Know we not the groundwork of the substance of our bodies?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Knowest thou not how from weak and shapeless and simple elements we are engendered, and out of what is simple and weak a living man is formed? and how that weak element being made flesh is changed into strong sinews, and bright eyes, and sensitive nose, and hearing ears, and speaking tongue, and beating heart, and busy hands, and swift feet, and into members of all kinds? and how that once weak element becomes a shipwright, and a builder, and an architect, and a craftsman of various arts, and a soldier, and a ruler, and a lawgiver, and a king?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Cannot God then, who has made us out of imperfect materials, raise us up when we have fallen into decay?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He who thus flames a body out of what is vile, cannot He raise the fallen body again?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And He who fashions that which is not, shall He not raise up that which is and is fallen?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Take further a manifest proof of the resurrection of the dead, witnessed month by month in the sky and its luminaries.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The body of the moon vanishes completely, so that no part of it is any more seen, yet it fills again, and is restored to its former state; and for the perfect demonstration of the matter, the moon at certain revolutions of years suffering eclipse and becoming manifestly changed into blood, yet recovers its luminous body: God having provided this, that thou also, the man who art formed of blood, mightest not refuse credence to the resurrection of the dead, but mightest believe concerning thyself also what thou seest in respect of the moon.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: These therefore use thou as arguments against the Greeks; for with them who receive not what is written fight thou with unwritten weapons, by reasonings only and demonstrations; for these men know not who Moses is, nor Esaias, nor the Gospels, nor Paul.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Turn now to the Samaritans, who, receiving the Law only, allow not the Prophets.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: To them the text just now read from Ezekiel appears of no force, for, as I said, they admit no Prophets; whence then shall we persuade the Samaritans also?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us go to the writings of the Law.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now God says to Moses, I am the God of Abraham, and of Isaac, and of Jacob; this must mean of those who have being and subsistence.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if Abraham has come to an end, and Isaac and Jacob, then He is the God of those who have no being.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When did a king ever say, I am the king of soldiers, whom he had not?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When did any display wealth which he possessed not?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Therefore Abraham and Isaac and Jacob must subsist, that God may be the God of those who have being; for He said not, “I was their God,” but I am.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And that there is a judgment, Abraham shews in saying to the Lord, He who judgeth all the earth, shall He not execute judgment?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But to this the foolish Samaritans object again, and say that the souls possibly of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob continue, but that their bodies cannot possibly rise again.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Was it then possible that the rod of righteous Moses should become a serpent, and is it impossible that the bodies of the righteous should live and rise again?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And was that done contrary to nature, and shall they not be restored according to nature?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Again, the rod of Aaron, though cut off and dead, budded, without the scent of waters, and though under a roof, sprouted forth into blossoms as in the fields; and though set in dry places, yielded in one night the flowers and fruit of plants watered for many years.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Did Aaron’s rod rise, as it were, from the dead, and shall not Aaron himself be raised?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And did God work wonders in wood, to secure to him the high-priesthood, and will He not vouchsafe a resurrection to Aaron himself?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A woman also was made salt contrary to nature; and flesh was turned into salt; and shall not flesh be restored to flesh?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Was Lot’s wife made a pillar of salt, and shall not Abraham’s wife be raised again?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: By what power was Moses’ hand changed, which even within one hour became as snow, and was restored again?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Certainly by God’s command.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Was then His command of force then, and has it no force now?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And whence in the beginning came man into being at all, O ye Samaritans, most senseless of all men?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Go to the first book of the Scripture, which even you receive; And God formed man of the dust of the ground.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is dust transformed into flesh, and shall not flesh be again restored to flesh?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: You must be asked too, whence the heavens had their being, and earth, and seas?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Whence sun, and moon, and stars?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: How from the waters were made the things which fly and swim?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And how from earth all its living things?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Were so many myriads brought from nothing into being, and shall we men, who bear God’s image, not be raised up?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Truly this course is full of unbelief, and the unbelievers are much to be condemned; when Abraham addresses the Lord as the Judge of all the earth, and the learners of the Law disbelieve; when it is written that man is of the earth, and the readers disbelieve it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: These questions, therefore, are for them, the unbelievers: but the words of the Prophets are for us who believe.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But since some who have also used the Prophets believe not what is written, and allege against us that passage, The ungodly shall not rise up in judgment, and, For if man go down to the grave he shall come up no more, and, The dead shall not praise Thee, O Lord,—for of what is well written, they have made ill use—it will be well in a cursory manner, and as far as is now possible, to meet them.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if it is said, that the ungodly shall not rise up in judgment, this shews that they shall rise, not in judgment, but in condemnation; for God needs not long scrutiny, but close on the resurrection of the ungodly follows also their punishment.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And if it is said, The dead shall not praise Thee, O Lord, this shews, that since in this life only is the appointed time for repentance and pardon, for which they who enjoy it shall praise the Lord, it remains not after death for them who have died in sins to give praise as the receivers of a blessing, but to bewail themselves; for praise belongs to them who give thanks, but to them who are under the scourge, lamentation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Therefore the just then offer praise; but they who have died in sins have no further season for confession.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And respecting that passage, If a man go down to the grave, he shall come up no more, observe what follows, for it is written, He shall come up no more, neither shall he return to his own house.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For since the whole world shall pass away, and every house shall be destroyed, how shall he return to his own house, there being henceforth a new and different earth?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But they ought to have heard Job, saying, For there is hope of a tree; for if it be cut down, it will sprout again, and the tender branch thereof will not cease.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For though the root thereof wax old in the earth, and the stock thereof die in the rocky ground; yet from the scent of water it will bud, and bring forth a crop like a new plant.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But man when he dies, is gone; and when mortal man falls, is he no more?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: As it were remonstrating and reproving (for thus ought we to read the words is no more with an interrogation); he says since a tree falls and revives, shall not man, for whom all trees were made, himself revive?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And that thou mayest not suppose that I am forcing the words, read what follows; for after saying by way of question, When mortal man falls, is he no more? he says, For if a man die, he shall live again; and immediately he adds, I will wait till I be made again; and again elsewhere, Who shall raise up on the earth my skin, which endures these things.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And Esaias the Prophet says, The dead men shall rise again, and they that are in the tombs shall awake.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the Prophet Ezekiel now before us, says most plainly, Behold I will open your graves, and bring you up out of your graves.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And Daniel says, Many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth shall arise, some to everlasting life, and some to everlasting shame.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And many Scriptures there are which testify of the Resurrection of the dead; for there are many other sayings on this matter.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But now, by way of remembrance only, we will make a passing mention of the raising of Lazarus on the fourth day; and just allude, because of the shortness of the time, to the widow’s son also who was raised, and merely for the sake of reminding you, let me mention the ruler of the synagogue’s daughter, and the rending of the rocks, and how there arose many bodies of the saints which slept, their graves having been opened.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But specially be it remembered that Christ has been raised from the dead.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I speak but in passing of Elias, and the widow’s son whom he raised; of Elisseus also, who raised the dead twice; once in his lifetime, and once after his death.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For when alive he wrought the resurrection by means of his own soul; but that not the souls only of the just might be honoured, but that it might be believed that in the bodies also of the just there lies a power, the corpse which was cast into the sepulchre of Elisseus, when it touched the dead body of the prophet, was quickened, and the dead body of the prophet did the work of the soul, and that which was dead and buried gave life to the dead, and though it gave life, yet continued itself among the dead.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Lest if Elisseus should rise again, the work should be ascribed to his soul alone; and to shew, that even though the soul is not present, a virtue resides in the body of the saints, because of the righteous soul which has for so many years dwelt in it, and used it as its minister.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And let us not foolishly disbelieve, as though this thing had not happened: for if handkerchiefs and aprons, which are from without, touching the bodies of the diseased, raised up the sick, how much more should the very body of the Prophet raise the dead?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And with respect to these instances we might say much, rehearsing in detail the marvellous circumstances of each event: but as you have been already wearied both by the superposed fast of the Preparation, and by the watchings, let what has been cursorily spoken concerning them suffice for a while; these words having been as it were sown thinly, that you, receiving the seed like richest ground, may in bearing fruit increase them.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But be it remembered, that the Apostles also raised the dead; Peter raised Tabitha in Joppa, and Paul raised Eutychus in Troas; and thus did all the other Apostles, even though the wonders wrought by each have not all been written.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Further, remember all the sayings in the first Epistle to the Corinthians, which Paul wrote against them who said, How are the dead raised, and with what manner of body do they come?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And how he says, For if the dead rise not, then is not Christ raised; and how he called them fools, who believed not; and remember the whole of his teaching there concerning the resurrection of the dead, and how he wrote to the Thessalonians, But we would not have you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning them which are asleep, that ye sorrow not, even as the rest which have no hope, and all that follows: but chiefly that, And the dead in Christ shall rise first.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But especially mark this, how very pointedly Paul says, For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For this body shall be raised not remaining weak as now; but raised the very same body, though by putting on incorruption it shall be fashioned anew,—as iron blending with fire becomes fire, or rather as He knows how, the Lord who raises us.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This body therefore shall be raised, but it shall abide not such as it now is, but an eternal body; no longer needing for its life such nourishment as now, nor stairs for its ascent, for it shall be made spiritual, a marvellous thing, such as we cannot worthily speak of.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then, it is said, shall the righteous shine forth as the sun, and the moon, and as the brightness of the firmament.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And God, fore-knowing men’s unbelief, has given to little worms in the summer to dart beams of light from their body, that from what is seen, that which is looked for might be believed; for He who gives in part is able to give the whole also, and He who made the worm radiant with light, will much more illuminate a righteous man.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We shall be raised therefore, all with our bodies eternal, but not all with bodies alike: for if a man is righteous, he will receive a heavenly body, that he may be able worthily to hold converse with Angels; but if a man is a sinner, he shall receive an eternal body, fitted to endure the penalties of sins, that he may burn eternally in fire, nor ever be consumed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And righteously will God assign this portion to either company; for we do nothing without the body.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We blaspheme with the mouth, and with the mouth we pray.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: With the body we commit fornication, and with the body we keep chastity.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: With the hand we rob, and by the hand we bestow alms; and the rest in like manner.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Since then the body has been our minister in all things, it shall also share with us in the future the fruits of the past.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Therefore, brethren, let us be careful of our bodies, nor misuse them as though not our own.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us not say like the heretics, that this vesture of the body belongs not to us, but let us be careful of it as our own; for we must give account to the Lord of all things done through the body.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Say not, none seeth me; think not, that there is no witness of the deed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Human witness oftentimes there is not; but He who fashioned us, an unerring witness, abides faithful in heaven, and beholds what thou doest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the stains of sin also remain in the body; for as when a wound has gone deep into the body, even if there has been a healing, the scar remains, so sin wounds soul and body, and the marks of its scars remain in all; and they are removed only from those who receive the washing of Baptism.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The past wounds therefore of soul and body God heals by Baptism; against future ones let us one and all jointly guard ourselves, that we may keep this vestment of the body pure, and may not for practising fornication and sensual indulgence or any other sin for a short season, lose the salvation of heaven, but may inherit the eternal kingdom of God; of which may God, of His own grace, deem all of you worthy.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thus much in proof of the Resurrection of the dead; and now, let me again recite to you the profession of the faith, and do you with all diligence pronounce it while I speak, and remember it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Faith which we rehearse contains in order the following, “And in one Baptism of repentance for the remission of sins; and in one Holy Catholic Church; and in the resurrection of the flesh; and in eternal life.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now of Baptism and repentance I have spoken in the earliest Lectures; and my present remarks concerning the resurrection of the dead have been made with reference to the Article “In the resurrection of the flesh.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now then let me finish what still remains to be said for the Article, “In one Holy Catholic Church,” on which, though one might say many things, we will speak but briefly.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It is called Catholic then because it extends over all the world, from one end of the earth to the other; and because it teaches universally and completely one and all the doctrines which ought to come to men’s knowledge, concerning things both visible and invisible, heavenly and earthly; and because it brings into subjection to godliness the whole race of mankind, governors and governed, learned and unlearned; and because it universally treats and heals the whole class of sins, which are committed by soul or body, and possesses in itself every form of virtue which is named, both in deeds and words, and in every kind of spiritual gifts.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And it is rightly named (Ecclesia) because it calls forth and assembles together all men; according as the Lord says in Leviticus, And make an assembly for all the congregation at the door of the tabernacle of witness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And it is to be noted, that the word assemble, is used for the first time in the Scriptures here, at the time when the Lord puts Aaron into the High-priesthood.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And in Deuteronomy also the Lord says to Moses, Assemble the people unto Me, and let them hear My words, that they may learn to fear Me.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And he again mentions the name of the Church, when he says concerning the Tables, And on them were written all the words which the Lord spake with you in the mount out of the midst of the fire in the day of the Assembly; as if he had said more plainly, in the day in which ye were called and gathered together by God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Psalmist also says, I will give thanks unto Thee, O Lord, in the great Congregation; I will praise Thee among much people.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Of old the Psalmist sang, Bless ye God in the congregations, even the Lord, (ye that are) from the fountains of Israel.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But after the Jews for the plots which they made against the Saviour were cast away from His grace, the Saviour built out of the Gentiles a second Holy Church, the Church of us Christians, concerning which he said to Peter, And upon this rock I will build My Church, and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And David prophesying of both these, said plainly of the first which was rejected, I have hated the Congregation of evil doers; but of the second which is built up he says in the same Psalm, Lord, I have loved the beauty of Thine house; and immediately afterwards, In the Congregations will I bless thee, O Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For now that the one Church in Judæa is cast off, the Churches of Christ are increased over all the world; and of them it is said in the Psalms, Sing unto the Lord a new song, His praise in the Congregation of Saints.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Agreeably to which the prophet also said to the Jews, I have no pleasure in you, saith the Lord Almighty; and immediately afterwards, For from the rising of the sun even unto the going down of the same, My name is glorified among the Gentiles.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Concerning this Holy Catholic Church Paul writes to Timothy, That thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the House of God, which is the Church of the Living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But since the word Ecclesia is applied to different things (as also it is written of the multitude in the theatre of the Ephesians, And when he had thus spoken, he dismissed the Assembly), and since one might properly and truly say that there is a Church of evil doers, I mean the meetings of the heretics, the Marcionists and Manichees, and the rest, for this cause the Faith has securely delivered to thee now the Article, “And in one Holy Catholic Church;” that thou mayest avoid their wretched meetings, and ever abide with the Holy Church Catholic in which thou wast regenerated.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And if ever thou art sojourning in cities, inquire not simply where the Lord’s House is (for the other sects of the profane also attempt to call their own dens houses of the Lord), nor merely where the Church is, but where is the Catholic Church.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For this is the peculiar name of this Holy Church, the mother of us all, which is the spouse of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Only-begotten Son of God (for it is written, As Christ also loved the Church and gave Himself for it, and all the rest,) and is a figure and copy of Jerusalem which is above, which is free, and the mother of us all; which before was barren, but now has many children.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For when the first Church was cast off, in the second, which is the Catholic Church, God hath set, as Paul says, first Apostles, secondly Prophets, thirdly teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, divers kinds of tongues, and every sort of virtue, I mean wisdom and understanding, temperance and justice, mercy and loving-kindness, and patience unconquerable in persecutions.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: She, by the armour of righteousness on the right hand and on the left, by honour and dishonour, in former days amid persecutions and tribulations crowned the holy martyrs with the varied and blooming chaplets of patience, and now in times of peace by God’s grace receives her due honours from kings and those who are in high place, and from every sort and kindred of men.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And while the kings of particular nations have bounds set to their authority, the Holy Church Catholic alone extends her power without limit over the whole world; for God, as it is written, hath made her border peace.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But I should need many more hours for my discourse, if I wished to speak of all things which concern her.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In this Holy Catholic Church receiving instruction and behaving ourselves virtuously, we shall attain the kingdom of heaven, and inherit eternal life; for which also we endure all toils, that we may be made partakers thereof from the Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For ours is no trifling aim, but our endeavour is for eternal life.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wherefore in the profession of the Faith, after the words, “And in the resurrection of the flesh,” that is, of the dead (of which we have discoursed), we are taught to believe also “in the life eternal,” for which as Christians we are striving.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The real and true life then is the Father, who through the Son in the Holy Spirit pours forth as from a fountain His heavenly gifts to all; and through His love to man, the blessings of the life eternal are promised without fail to us men also.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We must not disbelieve the possibility of this, but having an eye not to our own weakness but to His power, we must believe; for with God all things are possible.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And that this is possible, and that we may look for eternal life, Daniel declares, And of the many righteous shall they shine as the stars for ever and ever.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And Paul says, And so shall we be ever with the Lord: for the being for ever with the Lord implies the life eternal.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But most plainly of all the Saviour Himself says in the Gospel, And these shall go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous into life eternal.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And many are the proofs concerning the life eternal.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And when we desire to gain this eternal life, the sacred Scriptures suggest to us the ways of gaining it; of which, because of the length of our discourse, the texts we now set before you shall be but few, the rest being left to the search of the diligent.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They declare at one time that it is by faith; for it is written, He that believeth on the Son hath eternal life, and what follows; and again He says Himself, Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that heareth My word, and believeth Him that sent Me, hath eternal life, and the rest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: At another time, it is by the preaching of the Gospel; for He says, that He that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: At another time, by martyrdom and confession in Christ’s name; for He says, And he that hateth his life in this world, shall keep it unto life eternal.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again, by preferring Christ to riches or kindred; And every one that hath forsaken brethren, or sisters, and the rest, shall inherit eternal life.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Moreover it is by keeping the commandments, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not kill, and the rest which follow; as He answered to him that came to Him, and said, Good Master, what shall I do that I may have eternal life?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But further, it is by departing from evil works, and henceforth serving God; for Paul says, But now being made free from sin, and become servants to God, ye have your fruit unto sanctification, and the end eternal life.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the ways of finding eternal life are many, though I have passed over them by reason of their number.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the Lord in His loving-kindness has opened, not one or two only, but many doors, by which to enter into the life eternal, that, as far as lay in Him, all might enjoy it without hindrance.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thus much have we for the present spoken within compass concerning the life eternal, which is the last doctrine of those professed in the Faith, and its termination; which life may we all, both teachers and hearers, by God’s grace enjoy!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And now, brethren beloved, the word of instruction exhorts you all, to prepare your souls for the reception of the heavenly gifts.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: As regards the Holy and Apostolic Faith delivered to you to profess, we have spoken through the grace of the Lord as many Lectures, as was possible, in these past days of Lent; not that this is all we ought to have said, for many are the points omitted; and these perchance are thought out better by more excellent teachers.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But now the holy day of the Passover is at hand, and ye, beloved in Christ, are to be enlightened by the Laver of regeneration.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Ye shall therefore again be taught what is requisite, if God so will; with how great devotion and order you must enter in when summoned, for what purpose each of the holy mysteries of Baptism is performed, and with what reverence and order you must go from Baptism to the Holy Altar of God, and enjoy its spiritual and heavenly mysteries; that your souls being previously enlightened by the word of doctrine, ye may discover in each particular the greatness of the gifts bestowed on you by God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And after Easter’s Holy Day of salvation, ye shall come on each successive day, beginning from the second day of the week, after the assembly into the Holy Place of the Resurrection, and there, if God permit, ye shall hear other Lectures; in which ye shall again be taught the reasons of every thing which has been done, and shall receive the proofs thereof from the Old and New Testaments,—first, of the things done just before Baptism,—next, how ye were cleansed from your sins by the Lord, by the washing of water with the word,—and how like Priests ye have become partakers of the Name of Christ,—and how the Seal of the fellowship of the Holy Ghost was given to you,—and concerning the mysteries at the Altar of the New Testament, which have taken their beginning from this place, both what the Divine Scriptures have delivered to us, and what is the power of these mysteries, and how ye must approach them, and when and how receive them;—and at the end of all, how for the time to come ye must behave yourselves worthily of this grace both in words and deeds, that you may all be enabled to enjoy the life everlasting.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And these things shall be spoken, if it be God’s pleasure.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Finally, my brethren, rejoice in the Lord alway; again I will say, Rejoice: for your redemption hath drawn nigh, and the heavenly host of the Angels is waiting for your salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And there is now the voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord; and the Prophet cries, Ho, ye that thirst, come ye to the water; and immediately afterwards, Hearken unto me, and ye shall eat that which is good, and your soul shall delight itself in good things.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And within a little while ye shall hear that excellent lesson which says, Shine, shine, O thou new Jerusalem; for thy light is come.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Of this Jerusalem the prophet hath said, And afterwards thou shalt be called the city of righteousness, Zion, the faithful mother of cities; because of the law which went forth out of Zion, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem, which word has from hence been showered forth on the whole world.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: To her the Prophet also says concerning you, Lift up thine eyes round about, and behold thy children gathered together; and she answers, saying, Who are these that fly as a cloud, and as doves with their young ones to me? (clouds because of their spiritual nature, and doves, from their purity).

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again, she says, Who knoweth such things? or who hath seen it thus? did ever a land bring forth in one day? or was ever a nation born all at once? for as soon as Zion travailed, she brought forth her children.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And all things shall be filled with joy unspeakable because of the Lord who said, Behold, I create Jerusalem a rejoicing, and her people a joy.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And may these words be spoken now again over you also, Sing, O heavens, and be joyful, O earth; and then; for the Lord hath had mercy on His people, and comforted the lowly of His people.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And this shall come to pass through the loving-kindness of God, who says to you, Behold, I will blot out as a cloud thy transgressions, and as a thick cloud thy sins.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But ye who have been counted worthy of the name of Faithful (of whom it is written, Upon My servants shall be called a new name which shall be blessed on the earth,) ye shall say with gladness, Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who hath blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly places in Christ: in whom we have our redemption through His blood, the forgiveness of our sins, according to the riches of His grace, wherein He abounded towards us, and what follows; and again, But God being rich in mercy, for His great love wherewith He loved us, when we were dead through our trespasses, quickened us together with Christ, and the rest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again in like manner praise ye the Lord of all good things, saying, But when the kindness of God our Saviour, and His love towards man appeared, not by works of righteousness which we had done, but according to His mercy He saved us, through the washing of regeneration, and the renewing of the Holy Ghost, which He shed on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Saviour, that being justified by His grace, we might be made heirs, according to hope, of eternal life.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And may God Himself the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, give unto you a spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of Himself, the eyes of your understanding being enlightened, and may He ever keep you in good works, and words, and thoughts; to Whom be glory, honour, and power, through our Lord Jesus Christ, with the Holy Ghost, now and ever, and unto all the endless ages of eternity.
